articles.title: Oil Declines as US Says Indirect Talks With Iran Were Positive
articles.summary: Oil extended its biggest quarterly drop since the pandemic as traders monitored peace talks between the US and Iran and the return of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Stocks Fall, Dollar Climbs as Focus Turns to Warsh: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Equities started July on a cautious note as investors awaited comments from Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh at a time when traders are ramping up bets on US interest rate hikes. The dollar rose.

articles.title: Pakistan Inflation Cools, Keeping Central Bank on Guard
articles.summary: Pakistan’s inflation eased last month, though it remained in double digits, keeping policymakers on guard against persistent price pressures.

articles.title: Euro-Zone Inflation Slows More Than Expected as Oil Retreats
articles.summary: Euro-area inflation eased more than anticipated as efforts toward peace in the Middle East sent global energy prices lower.

articles.title: Gilt Market Is Giving Burnham a Pass For Now, Says Aegon’s Lynch

articles.title: ECB’s Kocher Says the Next Decisions Will Be Either Hike or Hold
articles.summary: The European Central Bank’s next policy decisions will come down to either hiking interest rates further or holding them steady, Governing Council member Martin Kocher said.

articles.title: EU's 'Historic' Steel Measures Will Help Preserve Jobs, Says Eurofer Director-General

articles.title: Germany’s Pension Fund Would Be Safe With Bundesbank, Nagel Says
articles.summary: Germany’s central bank is well positioned to oversee the country’s planned state-backed pension fund if it’s asked to do so, according to President Joachim Nagel.

articles.title: Galderma Slumps After FDA Turns Down Botox Rival Relfydess
articles.summary: Galderma Group AG shares slumped the most in more than a year after the US Food and Drug Administration turned down the Swiss dermatology firm’s rival Botox treatment Relfydess.
